title: Credential-holding CI jobs shipped without ref gate, tag-ancestry check, or pinned toolchain
summary: A deploy job with cloud secrets and a tag-triggered npm publish job with OIDC went to main missing three standard gates. An automated security review caught it post-merge; each gap was a one-line fix.
mistake: Wrote a workflow_dispatch deploy job and a tag-triggered publish job that hold secrets/id-token, forgetting that (1) workflow_dispatch executes the workflow definition from WHATEVER ref is chosen — a modified copy on any branch can print the secrets; (2) any v-tag, even on an unreviewed commit, would publish to the registry; (3) `npm install -g npm@latest` inside the credential-holding job is an unpinned supply-chain edge.
instead: Before merging any workflow whose job holds a secret or id-token, check three gates: (1) the job carries `if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'`; (2) tag-triggered publishes verify `git merge-base --is-ancestor $GITHUB_SHA origin/main` (full-history checkout); (3) every toolchain install in that job is pinned to an exact version AND its artifact checksum is verified before execution (sha256sum -c; use curl -fsSL so a failed download dies loudly). Review these BEFORE merge — security review after merge means the window was open.
